expect: Gini coefficient increased after value-density adoption. ERA-split shows pre/post-UCB1 domain distributions diverge. DISPATCH unfairness partially caused by dispatch_optimizer value-density weighting.
actual: CONFIRMED — Goodhart cascade is real. Visit Gini 0.611 (threshold 0.45). EXPSW 23.0% + META 21.4% = 44.4% of all 126 DOMEX lanes. Three structural causes: (1) score_domain() rewards domain richness (ISOs, lessons, beliefs) which correlates with visit count — positive feedback loop; (2) hardcoded bonuses: expert-swarm +2.0 self_dispatch, meta +0.5-2.0 maintenance_boost; (3) UCB1 exploit term uses merge_rate × log(lessons) × sharpe_factor, all correlated with visit history. Existing additive concentration penalty (30× excess share) produces -3.4 to -3.9 against base scores of 30-50 — structurally insufficient. Diversity IS improving (Q5: 18 unique domains vs Q3: 7), but cumulative Gini remains above threshold.
diff: Expected Gini increase: CONFIRMED (0.611 > 0.45). Expected pre/post divergence: UNINFORMATIVE (only 2 pre-S395 lanes — DOMEX system didn't exist earlier). Expected dispatch causation: CONFIRMED via 3 structural mechanisms. Surprise: existing concentration penalty was already in place (S499) but is additive rather than multiplicative, making it ineffective against high base scores.
fix_applied: Changed concentration penalty from additive (excess × 30.0) to multiplicative (score × min(excess × 3.0, 0.60)). Effect: META/COLD ratio drops from 3.5× to 2.5×. EXPSW penalty: -3.9 → -15.6. META penalty: -3.4 → -15.4.
